daily_fact: An electric-green stream flowing through Brooklyn Bridge Park is a positive sign: harmless fluorescent dye is helping engineers trace leaks and improve the park’s stormwater system.
topline_md: New York saw major transit and street-policy moves as the City Council backed fully subsidized Fair Fares for 1.3 million riders while state and local leaders pushed to curb highway expansion, improve air quality, and reform trash and streetscape management. Funding gaps and governance strains also stood out, from unfunded containerization and volunteer fire companies to a federal raid on a City Council member and efforts to avert health insurance losses for 450,000 residents.

civics_politics.title: Civics & Politics
civics_politics.summary_md:
### Civics & Politics

- Council backs expanding Fair Fares to fully cover subway and bus fares for 1.3 million low-income New Yorkers. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMixgFBVV95cUxQU1BSbGJseTF3bzcxZlo4VFpjcHVjTUlDWUNjVkRWUnc5X2dLY2J5SndoYURjbWNxSWROQXNmbjZqeVZSQXBpdDNmRUdwWmJpZ19vYi1qM0NTTWR1dDYySEhTSUZYZ2lBcGJIOVhvR0Vva1o1bl9Fbko0SHNZRnJRenVDQTctUjhJZnFxdDlyWU1iTXlKTEVIdHlBOW5vQjY5QnlFN0JZQ183Q180Z1gzeVVma0JtSGczU2oyS3hkTVFFLWxtZ0E?oc=5)
- New city plan aims to reduce sidewalk sheds and falling-debris hazards affecting streets, businesses, and pedestrians. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Federal agents raided a New York City Council member’s home, raising potential corruption and governance concerns at City Hall. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiywFBVV95cUxOVXpSV1hVQ2VGVzFWWHFXQkRZYTQ1Y3Zab1dNZk5OWnJxUGJWYktlc2U5NlVlcXFfS0xYQmtqdlhfaHdwZG5xZHRmU1NybmN3Vlh3dktaS2R6Wk14Q1BUUEFVRzU3c2x6VUhPdlAwRXZMTlNhclcxd2RiZVI1d3hpeWhBdXhQbFFQeTh3TF9qUGdYQklPWVpYcXhNMFFVOHRNdmtHTzBpd0VyM2cxM1diUTZsQzhadVc4YkxyMVB6M2lLYWR3QmJtVUFvSQ?oc=5)
- A state bill would limit highway expansions near vulnerable communities, with implications for Bronx and other city neighborhoods. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqwFBVV95cUxOVXlkQ3FYVy1jamY0c2lZanN5ZVozNFJvck5JRDNqNUpKdlJHcXkxMjdYRzlpaTdXaXJjSm1fRWZsSTA3U3pEZ0gwdFY3b0dqbUhXOGc2MFB5QVZnaHoxSlI4Nk01dHR0Q1U1WkczdEg3RDJiNGdmMjdmRF9XMDJkallaMGgwY2EzdnhleDJhallQNVcySGVfY0dRMTBpZzdMdS1tancwQ3V2TnM?oc=5)
- AOC is urging Hochul to drop a Cross Bronx Expressway widening plan that could affect nearby residents. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqAFBVV95cUxORl9IOUhkby1UVUQ3R3JDcUNuOEUzc0kwTGxMMm5UcnJ5N3VXamZITjdzMzJmRk9QX2JDZ0hsOUUxdXpMUzZSX2R2THRxOGVrYmRMZzJFVVZKR0ZDdWZHZGxBQzV5eFhZd1ZscmFzY1hGTTRONUttYjBLRC1mMkhSbGRKbE1LVEwzRUZTZEN3LW1ic1hpSFJYVXpnb2puXzhVbmUtdXZDLWc?oc=5)
- Council members could gain more control over community board appointments under a new bill. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMickFVX3lxTE5nY1Byek91MGlOejRYM1V6bkpPM0JRSzNsdmM5dF90aHJmaHV1SVhxbEo0WWlxb25KUFdOaUZzV0htMlFjNldpVkR2QWs5OS1kV3Vva1BCSXlRbklEcWpYcThQdVgzODFKUDBOT2l2dzlndw?oc=5)
- Roadway outdoor dining returns for 2026, but far fewer restaurants will qualify than under the pandemic-era program. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMitAFBVV95cUxNRW9TT2w0ZGJPRzY0NGRjSG0tSm9WSGlEVGs3SG4zUExkYmwyWDJVMDdRcjlHLXB1VVVVZ1BPZktEX1pYNUp6d0hkUHRWVmNjVmNHenc3b1dlSDNWSUc3RGRqRXJaQXJ6Qi1QTHdSWTZlQ2RlWGFQRWcxRlBjR3ZtLVhSa3VnNnNKTmpRWFVSTEt1cnRGOVloakpwWlp2dXFhaGtfTlZ2MnBJX19Odk1rY09OUzE?oc=5)
- Reforms to private trash hauling are reaching Manhattan’s Chinatown, potentially changing commercial pickup rules and street conditions. - Staten Island volunteer fire companies say they still lack city funding despite handling thousands of 911 calls. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- The city budget still lacks funding for trash containerization, delaying a major sanitation and street-cleanliness overhaul. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMirwFBVV95cUxNTzZPNkJvZUFMcXppV2EybUdEYURuTzRTY0hORGFPSHo5S0NROHoyM2RJa3pzRXNES2ZWT1ZHRVhscUJidUE4dnNsQkl3ZVg5ZUctY1lXV0VZTEg1R0NmZksxenYxUXllQnF2UVlNRFZOMHRjbTFyLVl4UElfdml6aFhkVzUwX1luM1U0SWpJdXJPc0hsNDdwb3Q5Vnh3N3RadWp3OTRsNHRncG1sSnlz?oc=5)
- Lawmakers warn moving the Army Corps office to New Jersey could delay New York infrastructure and resiliency projects. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijAFBVV95cUxNN1owY1hfQXNIXzJBUW56N0hBZ0hQb2RMZGwtMUhyRHpVcS1qekFIT3R1SUl5blNQYWhuUTBJMnV0VUlUOFo5T3hNcDNFemlJOTJNUjU0NFJQalNnM1hzb2EwUjlFYV9oV2J1ZkNOSk1HUzVDNG9JYXFoSVppUVlHLUlVbXh0bFMzWkkteA?oc=5)
- A proposed state bill would require two-person subway crews, drawing opposition from business and good-government groups. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMifkFVX3lxTFAwSmdMRVFOWXp4QmVuRDBtU3pWWlhMMlZPNThpQ1ZaV1phOFpwalRqVVJPTkdtR2RRVnFsMFBmNHZKNXlWbDV5NUJQS0VWQzVydG1aamFCaHpOWlJfRm9tdTdWWXZnNHVYVUlubWVjOC1qcW9waWtYZkZybEZFUQ?oc=5)
- Questions over unpaid fines and discounted settlements are clouding the city’s enforcement of illegal Airbnb rules. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMipwFBVV95cUxPc3JsU0FNME5sejVGM1ItNHJPb1FXQkdsWkJBWDN2LWZFbmtOQVNtR01uWDI2TWU1UTVOOHVMRTN0YUVIWmtFSGRWUmF5WmlqMzBBS3l0Z3MzN0ljQ3V0M1Jsb0VkdVROYjJaTHdGOWM5cXk2c3ZDVVcyTk5FeF9xUl9faUh1cW55bWFGZlB3Y01aVjJIYnV4UVFPOUZNV3lyT0xZS052Yw?oc=5)
